requirement: Full ticket hierarchy to leaf level — retrieve all child tickets recursively until childCount=0 at every branch
All linked tickets across all issue types (blocks/is blocked by/relates to/duplicates)
All referenced components labels epics and sprints
All comments referencing technical decisions or scope changes
All triggers on in-scope objects — note before/after and DML event
All handler classes and service classes called by those triggers — follow full call chain
All Queueable/Batch/Schedulable jobs that touch in-scope objects or fields
All @InvocableMethod and @AuraEnabled methods on in-scope objects
All callouts (HTTP/SOAP) initiated from in-scope trigger or handler chain
All Flows and Process Builders with record-triggered entry conditions on in-scope objects
All fault paths and error handlers within each identified Flow
All Subflows called from identified Flows — follow recursively
All Screen Flows referencing in-scope objects
All Price Rules referencing in-scope objects or fields — include conditions and actions
All Product Rules and their Filter/Action conditions
All Quote Calculator Plugin (QCP) methods that read or write in-scope fields
Full pricing waterfall for affected product families (list/discount/cost/markup)
All Configuration Attributes on affected Product Options
All Summary Variables referencing in-scope objects
All Validation Rules on in-scope objects — capture full formula logic
All cross-object references within those Validation Rule formulas
All Validation Rules on parent or child objects that reference in-scope fields
All inbound field mappings for in-scope objects (source field → SF field)
All outbound field mappings for in-scope objects (SF field → target field)
All Platform Events published or subscribed on in-scope objects
All Change Data Capture configurations for in-scope objects
All External IDs used for upsert operations on in-scope objects
All Profiles with object-level access on in-scope objects
All Profiles with Field-Level Security on in-scope fields
All Permission Sets and Permission Set Groups granting access to in-scope objects/fields
All Sharing Rules (criteria-based and ownership-based) on in-scope objects
All Apex-managed sharing on in-scope objects
All Custom Settings and Custom Metadata Types referenced in in-scope logic
All Dependent Picklist relationships involving in-scope fields
All Formula Fields on in-scope objects that reference other objects
All Roll-Up Summary Fields on parent objects aggregating in-scope child records
All LWC and Aura Components that reference in-scope objects or fields via wire/apex
All Visualforce Pages and Controllers referencing in-scope objects
All Reports and Report Types built on in-scope objects
All Dashboards using in-scope report data
Identify authoritative branch for analysis (main/master/release) — never analyse a feature branch as system baseline
Full recursive folder tree to leaf level — document complete directory structure before any file analysis begins
All Salesforce metadata files (.cls .trigger .flow .xml .component .page .trigger-meta.xml) across all subdirectories
All configuration files (.json .yaml .yml) in root and all subdirectories
All deployment scripts and CI/CD pipeline definition files
All open MRs/PRs related to in-scope Jira tickets or Salesforce objects
All merged MRs/PRs touching in-scope files in the last 90 days
All commit messages on in-scope files in the last 90 days — these frequently contain scope decisions not in Jira
All README files at every folder level — often contain undocumented conventions and architecture decisions
All cross-file class and trigger references — if a file references another file follow that reference recursively
All Confluence pages linked from any in-scope Jira ticket
All child pages of every identified Confluence page — traverse recursively to leaf level
All Confluence spaces containing documentation for in-scope Salesforce objects or processes
All architecture decision records (ADRs) or design documents referencing in-scope objects
All meeting notes or decision logs referencing in-scope tickets or objects in the last 90 days
All integration specifications or API documentation for in-scope integration touchpoints
All release notes or deployment runbooks referencing in-scope objects or tickets
All process flow diagrams or business process documentation for in-scope Salesforce processes
All onboarding or training documentation referencing in-scope objects — often reveals undocumented business rules
